name: Semaphore
def: A counter-based primitive permitting up to N concurrent acquisitions, blocking beyond that.
problem: Limit concurrent access to a pool of resources or signal availability between threads.
mechanism: acquire decrements (blocks at zero), release increments; a binary semaphore (N=1) acts like a lock; counting semaphores bound concurrency.
failures: Release without prior acquire corrupting the count
whenNot: Simple mutual exclusion (use a mutex with ownership)
myths: "Semaphore = mutex" — a mutex has ownership; a semaphore is a permit counter
